result.vlm_summary:
page_state:
Booking.com search results page for Phuket, showing 1 property found with filters “Wonderful: 9+” and “Resorts” applied.
task_relevant_visible_text:
“Phuket: 1 property found”; “Wonderful: 9+”; “Resorts”; “YUSU Hotel 羽宿酒店”; “Exceptional 9.6”; “Breakfast included”; “6 nights, 2 adults”; “Tue, May 5 — Mon, May 11”; “Beach Nearby”; “3,150 feet from beach”; “Per night $172”; “$1,034”.
selected_candidate:
YUSU Hotel 羽宿酒店
constraint_evidence:
- Phuket location: supported (“Phuket” in search bar and results header).
- Beach resort: not_visible (property is labeled “Resorts” filter, but no explicit “beach resort” classification shown).
- Guest rating above 8.5: supported (“Exceptional 9.6”).
- Available for 6-night stay: supported (“6 nights, 2 adults”; dates May 5–May 11 imply 6 nights).
- Stay from May 5, 2026: supported (search dates shown as Tue, May 5 — Mon, May 11; URL also has checkin=2026-05-05).
- Resort category: supported via active “Resorts” filter.
- Beach proximity/near beach: supported (“Beach Nearby”, “3,150 feet from beach”).
- Exact availability for selected dates: supported by visible listing and “See availability” button, but not fully confirmed until clicked.
action_target:
Clicking the svg element at lower-left likely targets a filter/control icon or map-related UI element near the sidebar.
success_signals:
A relevant filter/control opens or toggles; page changes without navigation error; results remain visible.
failure_signals:
No visible response;

observation.title: Booking.com: Hotels in Phuket. Book your hotel now!

evidence.claim: The final inspected state already shows a matching Phuket resort result with the required date range and rating: YUSU Hotel 羽宿酒店 is visible with “Exceptional 9.6”, “Resorts”, “Beach Nearby”, and dates “Tue, May 5 — Mon, May 11” / check-in 2026-05-05.

The run reached search results and then applied filters; the final visible state shows “Phuket: 1 property found”, so there is concrete evidence of a candidate satisfying the constraints rather than an obvious browser failure.

No concrete contradiction is visible in the inspected state; the only uncertainty is whether the browser-agent’s final click on a lower-left svg had any intended effect, but the page already displayed a plausible qualifying listing beforehand.
The task outcome is not explicitly failed by the available evidence, and the final page state is a valid end-state for the user’s search intent.

suggested_followups.label: Confirm selection
suggested_followups.message: If you need stronger evidence, inspect whether the YUSU Hotel card or its See availability page was clicked after the filtered results loaded.

suggested_followups.label: Check beach resort meaning
suggested_followups.message: If the task strictly requires an explicit beach resort label, verify whether Booking.com’s Resort filter and Beach Nearby are sufficient or if a beachfront property was needed.
